articles.title: Temperature in 11 states including MP, UP, Rajasthan nears 47°C: Snowfall in Kashmir & Uttarakhand; UP's Ba... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
All 50 of the world’s hottest cities in India:Mercury hits 47.6°C in Banda; temp touches 40-47°C across 11 states including MP, R'than
India is unlikely to receive any relief from the intense heat over the next two days, with temperatures across 11 northern, western and central states ranging between 40°C and 47°C on Friday. States affected include Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan.
A heatwave alert has been issued for all 11 states. The highest temperatures were recorded in Banda, Uttar Pradesh, at 47.6°C, and Naugaon, Madhya Pradesh, at 46.8°C.
All 50 hottest cities in the world are in India
An unusual record was registered in the country on Friday morning. All the cities in the list of the world's 50 hottest cities were from India. Many cities in Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra, and Haryana reached 42 to 44 °C even before noon. 22 cities from Uttar Pradesh alone were on the list of hot cities.
According to AQI, Balangir in Odisha was the hottest city at 10:50 AM with 45 °C. By afternoon, Brahmapuri in Vidarbha recorded the country's highest temperature of 47.2 °C. At 3 PM, the mercury in the world's 22 hottest cities was above 47 °C. All these cities were in India.
Lightning kills 4 in Bihar, rain in J&K
Rain lashed several districts of Bihar, including Araria, Jehanabad, Nalanda and Supaul, on Friday, while lightning strikes claimed four lives in the state. Snowfall was also reported in Zojila in Jammu and Kashmir, as well as in Chamoli and Pithoragarh districts of Uttarakhand.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), one cyclonic circulation is currently active over Pakistan, while another has formed near Bihar. A low-pressure area has also developed over the Arabian Sea.
Due to these weather systems, conditions are expected to change across several parts of the country on Saturday. Rainfall is likely in Delhi, West Bengal, the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, and Lakshadweep, while Maharashtra and Goa may also receive showers.
Weather pictures from across the country
Weather forecast for the next two daysMay 24:
- Heatwave conditions may persist in Bihar, Delhi, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Punjab, Rajasthan, and Telangana. Hot and humid weather may persist in some parts of West Bengal, Odisha, and Tamil Nadu.
- Heavy rainfall may occur in Kerala, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu. A thunderstorm warning with winds gusting up to 50-60 kmph has been issued for Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, and Jharkhand.
May 25:
- Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, and Rajasthan will experience heat and heatwave conditions; the sky will be clear and the sun intense. Meanwhile, southern states like Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Assam, Meghalaya are expected to experience rain and strong winds with lightning.
Weather conditions of the states
Madhya Pradesh: Heatwave alert today in 42 districts of the state, mercury at 46.8°C in Nowgong, 46.4°C in Khajuraho
Today, a heatwave alert has been issued for 42 districts of Madhya Pradesh. Gwalior-Chambal, Sagar, Rewa, and Ujjain divisions are in the grip of the heatwave. There is a red alert in Tikamgarh, Chhatarpur, and Panna, an orange alert in 21 districts, including Gwalior, and a yellow alert in 18 districts.
Uttar Pradesh: Red alert for heatwave in 4 districts and Orange alert in 18 districts of the state; Banda's temperature 46.4°C
People in Uttar Pradesh are not getting relief from the heat. On Friday too, Banda district remained the hottest city in the state, with a maximum temperature of 46.441.0 °C°C. According to the Meteorological Department, today there is a red alert for heatwave in 4 districts: Banda, Chitrakoot, Kaushambi, and Prayagraj. An orange alert will remain in 18 districts.
